title: Paper-guided stronger alignment and reconstruction loss

queries: reconstructive graph instruction tuning graph token alignment loss llm
graph-aware language model alignment supervision reconstruction auxiliary loss

papers.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
papers.claim: Explicit reconstruction-style graph supervision improves graph-token alignment beyond text-only objectives.
papers.relevance: Directly motivates adding a stronger auxiliary graph alignment / reconstruction loss instead of only retuning basis mixing coefficients.

papers.title: Graph-Aware Language Model Pre-Training on a Large Graph Corpus Can Help Multiple Graph Applications
papers.claim: Graph-aware pretraining and auxiliary graph-text objectives can improve downstream transfer across multiple graph tasks.
papers.relevance: Supports moving upstream toward representation supervision rather than another prompt-side scan.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Alignment quality is the key bottleneck in graph soft prompting.
papers.relevance: Supports treating alignment loss design as the next central variable.

evidence.why_now: Basis-side, retrieval-side, and pretrain-warmup routes have all failed to deliver a non-regressive bundle. The pretrain warmup path is additionally blocked by an implementation issue, so the next productive slot is an explicit code-backed alignment/reconstruction loss change.

hypothesis: The existing basis losses are too weak and too indirect to stabilize the graph-text interface across tasks. Adding a stronger auxiliary alignment/reconstruction objective to the current fine-tuning path should be more promising than continuing to tune basis coefficients or relying on the broken pretrain warmup path.
notes: This candidate requires code changes before render/launch.
Intended scope: extend the current basis alignment path with a stronger explicit reconstruction or latent-alignment objective.
This is a finetune-only candidate; do not use pretrain.
Implementation is now available through new basis_lambda_tok_recon and basis_lambda_g_recon CLI knobs.
Launched on 2026-05-06 under the refreshed 512-step strict baseline.
Completed on 2026-05-06 with global verdict: failed.
user-churn improved, but user-ltv and item-incoterms both regressed against the refreshed 512-step strict baselines.
